message:
chore: update README architecture docs, promote FUSE smoke tests, and clean dep manifests

- README.md: Clarify harness→core dependency, add repair-wiring lifecycle
  note, correct dependency section (workspace deps use crates.io releases,
  not sibling checkouts), and simplify write-path diagram
- fuse_e2e.rs: Promote read-only smoke tests (fuse_read_hello_txt,
  fuse_readdir_root) from #[ignore] to default execution — they now
  skip gracefully when prerequisites are unavailable
- Remove unused dependencies from ffs-dir, ffs-ondisk, ffs-xattr manifests
- Update Cargo.lock for removed deps
- ffs-ondisk/lib.rs: Minor import/module adjustments
- Update beads issue tracker

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-huh.6","title":"Add comprehensive unit tests for ext4 write path invariants","description":"# Add comprehensive unit tests for ext4 write path invariants

## GOAL
Verify all invariants of the ext4 write path: extent tree mutation, block allocation, journal commit, inode updates.

## DELIVERABLES
1. Extent tree mutation tests:
   - Insert extent, split node, verify tree invariants
   - Delete extent, merge nodes, verify tree invariants
   - Truncate: remove extents beyond size
2. Block allocation tests:
   - Allocate in preferred group, verify bitmap updated
   - Allocate when preferred group full, verify fallback
   - Free blocks, verify bitmap cleared
   - Contiguous allocation preference
3. Journal commit tests:
   - Transaction bracket: begin → operations → commit
   - Rollback: begin → operations → abort → verify no changes
   - Crash simulation: commit interrupted, verify recovery
4. Inode update tests:
   - Size update after write
   - Timestamp update (mtime, ctime)
   - Link count update after link/unlink
   - Checksum recalculation

## TESTS (≥25 individual test cases)

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. ≥25 unit tests for ext4 write path
2. All tree/allocation invariants verified
3.  {"id":"bd-kro","title":"Implement full COW block rewrite path with allocation","description":"# Implement full COW block rewrite path with allocation

## GOAL
Complete the COW block rewrite path so writes allocate new blocks instead of overwriting, enabling true version chain semantics.

## BACKGROUND
From FEATURE_PARITY.md: "COW block rewrite path: 🟡 Basic version copy only"

Current state: Versions are created but may reuse same block location.
Required: Writes MUST allocate new blocks, old blocks retained for active snapshots.

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. COW Write Flow
```rust
impl MvccStore {
    pub fn write_cow(
        &self,
        block: BlockNumber,
        data: &[u8],
        txn: &Transaction,
        allocator: &dyn BlockAllocator,
        cx: &Cx,
    ) -> Result<BlockNumber> {
        // 1. Check if block has active snapshot references
        // 2. If yes: allocate new block, write there
        // 3. If no: could rewrite in place (optimization)
        // 4. Add to transaction write set with new location
        // 5. Return new block number
    }
}
```

### 2. Logical-to-Physical Mapping
- Logical block numbers stable across versions
- Physical locations vary (new allocation per write)
- Extent trees must be updated with new physical locations

### 3. Free Space Tracking
```rust
pub trait CowAllocator {
    /// Allocate block for COW write
    fn alloc_cow(&self, hint: Option<BlockNumber>, cx: &Cx) -> Result<BlockNumber>;
    
    /// Mark block as freeable when no references
    fn defer_free(&self, block: BlockNumber, commit_seq: CommitSeq);
    
    /// Actually free blocks older than watermark
    fn gc_free(&self, watermark: CommitSeq, cx: &Cx) -> usize;
}
```

### 4. GC Integration
- Blocks freed only when no snapshot references
- Watermark = oldest active snapshot
- Freed blocks returned to allocator

## TESTS
1. Unit: COW write allocates new block
2. Unit: Old block readable via old snapshot
3. Integration: 100 writes to same logical block create 100 physical blocks
4. Integration: GC frees blocks when no refs
5. Property: Block count = active versions + overhead

## LOGGING
- COW allocation (trace): logical, old_physical, new_physical
- Defer free (trace): block, commit_seq
- GC (debug): freed count, watermark

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Writes never overwrite blocks with active snapshot refs
2. Logical block numbers stable
3. GC reclaims space correctly
4.  {"id":"bd-xzn0","title":"Succinct structures for free-space bitmaps (Graveyard Entry 7.1)","description":"# Succinct structures for free-space bitmaps (Graveyard Entry 7.1)

## GOAL
Replace traditional free-space bitmaps with succinct data structures (rank/select bitmaps) that support O(1) rank and select queries while using near-optimal space.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard 7.1: Succinct bitmaps (Jacobson 1989, Clark 1996) store bitmaps in n + o(n) bits while supporting:
- rank(i): count set bits in positions [0, i) — O(1)
- select(k): find position of k-th set bit — O(1)

For free-space bitmaps, this enables:
- O(1) "how many free blocks in range?" (rank)
- O(1) "find k-th free block" (select) — replaces linear scan for allocation

## DESIGN
- Two-level rank structure: superblocks (every 2^16 bits) + blocks (every 2^9 bits)
- Select via rank binary search or Vigna's select structure
- Space overhead: ~6.25% over raw bitmap (vs 0% for plain bitmap, but queries are O(n))
- Integration: replace ffs-ext4 block bitmap and inode bitmap

## APPLICABILITY
- Optimization for large filesystems where bitmap scans are expensive
- Most impactful when block groups are large or filesystem is >90% full
- Could also accelerate mballoc buddy allocator queries

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Succinct bitmap with O(1) rank and select
- [ ] Space overhead < 10% over raw bitmap
- [ ] Benchmark: allocation scan time vs plain bitmap
- [ ] Drop-in replacement for block/inode bitmaps","acceptance_criteria":"Succinct bitmap with O(1) rank and select operations. Space overhead below 10% over raw bitmap. Two-level rank structure: superblocks (every 2^16 bits) plus blocks (every 2^9 bits). Drop-in replacement for block and inode bitmaps via BlockBitmap trait. Benchmark: allocation scan time vs plain bitmap at 50%, 80%, 95% fullness. Benchmark: rank/select latency vs linear scan. Unit tests: rank correctness for all-zeros, all-ones, random patterns. Unit tests: select correctness for sparse and dense bitmaps. Unit tests: round-trip serialization/deserialization.  FrankenFS is a 21-crate Cargo workspace (19 core crates + 2 legacy/reference wrappers) with a strict DAG dependency graph:
+`ffs-harness` links directly against `ffs-core` for conformance/perf surfaces, and `ffs-cli` depends on both `ffs-core` and `ffs-harness`.

 - **FUSE delegates to ffs-core.** `ffs-fuse` maps FUSE protocol to `ffs-core::FrankenFsEngine` — it contains no filesystem logic.
 - **Repair is orthogonal.** `ffs-repair` operates on blocks, not files. It doesn't know about inodes or directories.
+- **Repair wiring is lifecycle-based.** `ffs-core` reaches repair functionality via `ffs-mvcc`/block flush integration rather than a direct `ffs-core -> ffs-repair` dependency edge.
